judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all five expectations with equivalent, well-cited coverage; they are comparable.

report_1.evidence: Per `incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md`, failures began 14:03 and the FIRST ALERT fired at 14:50 — a 47-minute detection gap, not "minutes."

report_2.evidence: `OPS-412-timeline.md`: failures began 14:03, the FIRST ALERT fired at 14:50 (a 47-minute detection gap)

report_1.evidence: `incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md` states "1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing; 37 were unrecoverable and refunded."

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md` states: "During the window 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing; 37 were unrecoverable and refunded."

report_1.evidence: `metrics/uptime-2026.csv` shows `2026-04,99.77`. `docs/sla-commitments.md` states the contractual monthly SLA is 99.9% and "Any calendar month below 99.9% is a breach and owes service credits per the customer agreement... April at 99.77% is a breach

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/metrics/uptime-2026.csv` shows April 2026 uptime of `99.77`. `larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md`: "The contractual MONTHLY uptime SLA is 99.9%... April (99.77%) is below 99.9%, so this incident put the month into contractual breach

report_1.evidence: Draft line 12–13: "The outage was caused by the on-call engineer deploying an untested config change." `docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 1: "Never assign blame to an individual." Reframe to a process/systemic cause.

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 1: "Never assign blame to an individual" — this sentence pins the outage on a named role/person... This sentence must be rewritten in system-level, blameless terms

report_1.evidence: The draft leads with reputation defense, not customer impact — violates style-guide rule 3... Rule 3 requires leading with customer impact and remediation. The structure must be reordered to open with what happened to customers and what LarkPay is doing about it.

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 3: "Lead with customer impact and remediation." The draft leads with "99.99% uptime … our commitment to reliability"... It must be reordered to lead with what happened to customers and what LarkPay is doing about it.
